frameworks.name: default-mode network / self-projection
frameworks.one_line: narrative engages self-projection and theory-of-mind circuits in DMN

frameworks.name: inter-subject neural synchronization
frameworks.one_line: shared neural responses across listeners index narrative impact and propagation

frameworks.name: reward-based valuation
frameworks.one_line: reward circuits assign emotional valence to narrative content

core_question: How do narratives shape perception, cognition, emotion, and decision-making in the human brain, and how does individual processing relate to cultural propagation?
key_assumptions: narratives recruit a distributed network rather than a single language module
inter-subject synchronization indexes shared cognitive processing of narrative content
neural signatures of individual processing can predict population-level spread
manipulations: stimulus type across narrative formats (stories, audiobooks, films, media feeds)
between-subjects comparison via inter-subject correlation analyses
key_findings: narratives engage default-mode regions for self-projection and theory of mind
language areas support semantic processing of narrative content
reward circuits contribute to emotional valuation during narrative
inter-subject synchronization signatures predict cultural propagation
interpretations: narrative processing links a distributed brain network to downstream behavioral and cultural influence
neural synchrony across individuals is a candidate biomarker for which narratives go viral
